investments.business_description: ERA Industries (operating as BTX Precision) is a precision aerospace machining and manufacturing company that produces complex structural components and assemblies for aerospace and defense programs. The company specializes in tight-tolerance machined parts from aluminum, titanium, and other advanced alloys for commercial and military aircraft. BTX Precision serves as a Tier 2 supplier to major aerospace OEMs and prime contractors.
investments.end_customers: End customers are major aerospace OEMs (Boeing, Airbus), defense prime contractors (Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman), and Tier 1 aerospace suppliers. They source precision machined components from BTX for use in airframes, engine housings, and structural assemblies where tight tolerances and material certifications are critical.

investments.business_description: Turbine Engine Specialists, Inc. (TES) is a business and general aviation turbine engine MRO specialist, focusing on inspection, overhaul, repair, and parts distribution for turboprop and turbofan engines used in corporate jets and turboprop aircraft. The company provides FAA-certified repair capabilities for engines such as Pratt & Whitney Canada PT6 series and Williams engines. TES serves as an authorized service center for multiple engine OEMs.
investments.end_customers: End customers are corporate flight departments, charter operators, regional airlines, and turboprop aircraft operators requiring engine overhauls, hot section inspections, and component repair. Operators choose TES for its engine-specific expertise, OEM authorizations, and turnaround times critical to flight schedule reliability.

investments.business_description: Randys Holdings, Inc. is the holding company for RANDYS Worldwide Automotive, a leading U.S. distributor and manufacturer of highly engineered, branded automotive drivetrain, suspension, and undercar aftermarket products focused on SUVs, trucks, and CUVs. The company's key brands — USA Standard Gear, Yukon Gear & Axle, and Zumbrota Drivetrain — cover OE-replacement and performance differential gears, axles, lockers, limited slips, transfer cases, manual transmissions, and driveshafts. RANDYS is headquartered in Everett, Washington, and operates distribution centers across seven states, backed by Tailwind Capital.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are automotive parts retailers, professional speed shops, 4WD and off-road specialty retailers, and professional installers — including both national chains and independent repair shops — that serve truck, SUV, and performance vehicle owners seeking drivetrain repair, replacement, and performance upgrades. Fleet and commercial vehicle operators also purchase RANDYS' products for drivetrain maintenance.

investments.business_description: Patriot Growth Insurance Services is a growth-focused national insurance brokerage platform founded in January 2019 by CEO Matt Gardner, a 30-year insurance distribution veteran, and backed initially by Summit Partners and subsequently recapitalized by GI Partners in September 2021. Patriot has rapidly grown to a top-25 U.S. insurance agency through partnerships with employee benefits and property and casualty agencies, completing 64+ agency partnerships and building a footprint across 18 states serving over 100,000 clients. The firm focuses on partnering with agency owners who want to grow while retaining autonomy.
investments.end_customers: End customers are businesses and individuals who purchase employee benefits, group health insurance, and property and casualty insurance through Patriot's network of local agency partners. Patriot's model particularly targets businesses seeking employee benefits expertise — health, dental, vision, disability, and voluntary benefits — alongside commercial insurance coverage.

investments.business_description: Commercial Bakeries Corp. is a contract manufacturer of baked goods — including cookies, crackers, waffles, and frozen bakery items — for private-label and branded food companies. The company operates large-scale bakery manufacturing facilities and produces products to customer specifications for major food brands, retailers, and foodservice companies. Commercial Bakeries specializes in high-volume, consistent production of shelf-stable and frozen bakery items.

investments.business_description: Sugar PPC Buyer LLC is the acquisition vehicle used by Pritzker Private Capital (PPC) to acquire Sugar Foods Corporation in 2023. Sugar Foods, headquartered in Westlake Village, California and founded in 1948, is a leading producer of crunchy toppings, croutons (brands include Fresh Gourmet and Mrs. Cubbison's), beverage ingredients, pizza toppings, baking mixes, and confectionery ingredients for foodservice, retail, and private label channels. The company operates five processing facilities across the U.S. and Mexico and employs more than 1,400 people, serving over 3,000 customers.
investments.end_customers: Sugar Foods sells to major grocery retailers, foodservice distributors, quick-service restaurants, and casual dining chains globally. Its products appear on salad bars and restaurant tables as private-label and branded toppings, and in home kitchens via retail grocery. Customers value Sugar Foods for its broad product range, national manufacturing footprint, and the ability to deliver both branded and private-label solutions at scale.

investments.business_description: Watermill Express is the largest drive-up pure drinking water and ice vending franchise in the United States, founded in 1984 and headquartered in Brighton, Colorado. The company operates over 1,300 self-serve outdoor kiosks across more than a dozen states, providing 24/7 purified water through a patented 12-step purification process that includes reverse osmosis and ultraviolet disinfection. Brentwood Associates is the primary institutional backer, having partnered with the founding Dolifka family.
investments.end_customers: Watermill Express serves everyday consumers seeking high-quality purified drinking water at a fraction of the cost of bottled water, with kiosks typically located in grocery store parking lots and strip mall forecourts. Customers are price-conscious households — particularly in the Sun Belt — who refill reusable jugs rather than buying single-use plastic bottles. The business model appeals strongly to value-oriented and environmentally aware consumers.

investments.business_description: Engineered Fastener Company LLC (EFC International) is a St. Louis-based global distributor and application engineer of specialty fasteners, panel fasteners, and engineered assembly components, founded in 1983 and owned by Frontenac. EFC supplies injection-molded plastic components, spring steel stampings, cold-formed fasteners, clamps, and motion control parts to automotive, HVAC, medical, and industrial OEMs worldwide.
investments.end_customers: Customers are OEM engineers and manufacturers in automotive, HVAC, medical device, and industrial equipment sectors who source EFC's custom-engineered fasteners and assembly solutions for use in complex assemblies requiring precise, reliable fit and function.

investments.business_description: Hyperion Materials & Technologies is a global manufacturer of tungsten carbide, polycrystalline diamond (PCD), and advanced hard material products used in industrial cutting, drilling, and wear applications. The company produces carbide cutting tools, wear parts, mining inserts, and oil and gas drill bits for demanding industrial applications requiring extreme hardness and wear resistance. Hyperion serves as a critical materials supplier across metal cutting, mining, oil and gas, and construction drilling industries.
investments.end_customers: End customers are cutting tool manufacturers, mining equipment producers, oil and gas drill bit manufacturers, and industrial manufacturers that use hard material components in production processes. End users of the ultimate products include metalworking manufacturers, mining companies, oil and gas drillers, and construction companies that depend on the wear performance of carbide-tipped tools and components.

investments.business_description: Ovation Holdings is the parent of a flow control MRO (maintenance, repair, and operations) distribution business, supplying valves, actuators, instrumentation, and flow control products to industrial facilities. The company distributes products from major valve and instrumentation manufacturers while also providing repair, calibration, and field services for installed flow control equipment. Ovation serves the chemical, refining, power, and general industrial markets.
investments.end_customers: End customers are process industry facilities including chemical plants, oil refineries, power generation stations, and water treatment facilities that require ongoing supply and maintenance of flow control equipment. Plant maintenance engineers and procurement managers source from Ovation for its technical expertise, inventory availability, and valve repair capabilities that minimize unplanned downtime.

investments.business_description: Thermostat Purchaser III, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle for CoolSys (doing business as Reedy Industries), an Ares Management-backed commercial HVAC and refrigeration services company. investments.business_description: Air Canada 2020-2 Class B Pass Through Trust is an Enhanced Equipment Trust Certificate (EETC) special purpose vehicle created by Air Canada to raise secured financing against specific aircraft in its fleet. Class B certificates represent subordinated debt tranches within the EETC structure, sitting junior to Class AA and Class A tranches but senior to equity, and are secured by the aircraft equipment notes. EETCs are a standard aircraft financing structure used by airlines globally to access capital markets at lower costs than unsecured debt.
investments.end_customers: The 'customers' of this structured product are institutional fixed-income investors—insurance companies, pension funds, and asset managers—who purchase aviation asset-backed securities seeking yield above investment-grade corporate bonds, with collateral protection from the underlying aircraft. Air Canada as the issuing airline benefits from lower-cost secured financing versus unsecured bonds, using the aircraft as collateral to appeal to investors requiring recoverable assets in a bankruptcy scenario.
